as: Provide information about historical changes in cash.
indAs: Provide information about historical changes in cash and cash equivalents.
ifrs: Provide information about historical changes in cash and cash equivalents.
usGaap: Provide information about cash receipts and cash payments.
logicShift: Consistent across all frameworks.

as: Exemptions exist for SMCs and certain non-corporate entities.
indAs: Mandatory for all entities preparing financial statements under Ind AS.
logicShift: Universal application in modern frameworks.

indAs: Operating, Investing, Financing.
logicShift: Consistent core structure across all frameworks.

as: Short-term, highly liquid investments convertible to known cash.
indAs: Short-term, highly liquid investments readily convertible to known cash.
ifrs: Short-term, highly liquid investments readily convertible to known cash.
usGaap: Similar, but explicit rules on restricted cash.
logicShift: Generally aligned across standards.

as: Classified as financing liabilities. Not part of cash equivalents.
indAs: Included in cash equivalents if repayable on demand and form an integral part of cash management.
ifrs: Included in cash equivalents if repayable on demand and form an integral part of cash management.
usGaap: Excluded from cash equivalents. Treated as financing activities.
logicShift: Significant difference between IFRS/Ind AS and US GAAP/AS 3.

indAs: Judgment required; generally excluded from cash equivalents if restricted.
ifrs: Judgment required; generally excluded from cash equivalents if restricted.
usGaap: Must be included in the total cash balance shown in the cash flow statement; reconciled to balance sheet.
logicShift: US GAAP is prescriptive; IFRS/Ind AS require judgment.

basisOfComparison: Operating activities - Direct vs Indirect method
indAs: Direct method explicitly encouraged, but Indirect permitted.
usGaap: Direct method encouraged, Indirect permitted (if Direct used, reconciliation to net income required).
logicShift: Indirect method is the practical standard globally.

indAs: Profit or loss (flexible).
logicShift: US GAAP is strict on using Net Income.

indAs: Operating OR Financing activity (policy choice).
logicShift: Major comparability issue across standards and peers.

indAs: Operating OR Investing activity (policy choice).
logicShift: US GAAP includes all interest in operating.

indAs: Financing OR Operating activity (policy choice).
logicShift: IFRS/Ind AS flexibility allows variation.

indAs: Operating OR Investing activity (policy choice).
logicShift: US GAAP treats as operating return on investment.

indAs: Operating activity unless specifically identifiable with investing/financing.
ifrs: Operating activity unless specifically identifiable with investing/financing.
logicShift: US GAAP bans allocation to investing/financing.

as: Classified according to nature and separately disclosed.
indAs: Prohibited.
logicShift: Elimination of extraordinary items globally.

indAs: Principal = Financing; Interest = Operating/Financing.
usGaap: Operating lease = Operating activity; Finance lease = Financing (principal) & Operating (interest).
logicShift: US GAAP operating leases remain in operating cash flows.

as: Recorded at exchange rate on transaction date.
indAs: Recorded at exchange rate on transaction date; unrealized gains/losses separately presented.
ifrs: Recorded at exchange rate on transaction date; unrealized gains/losses separately presented.
logicShift: Consistent approach.

indAs: Excluded; disclosed elsewhere.
logicShift: Consistent principle.

basisOfComparison: Changes in ownership interests in subsidiaries (no loss of control)
indAs: Financing activity.
logicShift: Aligned. Transactions with owners in capacity as owners.

indAs: Investing activity (net of cash acquired/disposed).
logicShift: Consistent logic.

indAs: Include only cash flows between investor and investee (e.g., dividends).
ifrs: Include only cash flows between investor and investee (e.g., dividends).
usGaap: Include only cash flows between investor and investee (e.g., dividends).
logicShift: Consistent focus on actual cash movements.

indAs: Generally Investing (if related to original transaction/liability).
ifrs: Generally Investing (if related to original transaction/liability).
usGaap: Operating (if soon after acquisition - <= 3 months) or Financing (later).
logicShift: US GAAP is prescriptive and different.

basisOfComparison: Disclosure of changes in financing liabilities
indAs: Explicitly required (roll-forward of debt/lease liabilities, separating cash and non-cash changes).
usGaap: Not explicitly required as a single roll-forward, but expected via other disclosures.
logicShift: IFRS/Ind AS requirement adds significant transparency.

indAs: High.
logicShift: Modern frameworks demand more data.

indAs: Classified in same category as the cash flows of the hedged item.
ifrs: Classified in same category as the cash flows of the hedged item.
usGaap: Generally same as Ind AS/IFRS, but some complex exceptions.
logicShift: Consistent matching principle.
